pi_items.source: Web search: FLIPCHART files are the native file format for ActivInspire (https://file-extensions.com/docs/flipchart, https://fileinfo.com/extension/flipchart). Task_utils.sh confirms .flipchart is the primary extension for ActivInspire.

Setup_task.sh creates this exact directory path (mkdir -p /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts). This follows standard Linux home directory conventions. Task_utils.sh default path for list_flipcharts is /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts.
Task description in task.json explicitly states: 'Create a 2-page custom weather tracking flipchart'. Page 1 is Dashboard, Page 2 is Weekly Log.

pi_items.verified_value: ["0", "32", "50", "70", "100"] - Valid reference points: 0°C (freezing in Celsius), 32°F (freezing in Fahrenheit), 70°F (room temperature), 100°C (boiling in Celsius)
pi_items.source: Web search on temperature scales: 0°C = freezing point of water, 100°C = boiling point of water, 32°F = freezing point in Fahrenheit, 70°F ≈ 21°C (comfortable room temperature). These are real, standard reference points used in thermometer diagrams.
Task description explicitly states: 'Add weather condition text: "Sunny" and "Rainy"'. Verifier checks for both with grep case-insensitive.

pi_items.verified_value: ["Mon", "Wed", "Fri"] - NOTE: Verifier only checks for Mon, Wed, Fri; Tue and Thu are not verified by the scoring script
pi_items.source: Task description states: 'Add headers for the school week: "Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri". Metadata includes all 5 days. However, export_result.sh only sets HAS_MON, HAS_WED, HAS_FRI flags (lines 34-36), and verifier.py line 101 checks only ['mon', 'wed', 'fri']. This is an inconsistency between metadata claim and verifier implementation.

Task description and verifier both require: Circle (for thermometer bulb), Rectangle (for thermometer stem), Line (for weekly log structure). These are standard drawing tools available in ActivInspire as confirmed by shape recognition documentation.

pi_items.metadata_value: Using Shape tool: Circle (bulb) and Rectangle (stem)
pi_items.verified_value: Shape tool available in ActivInspire (Professional Version required). Circle and Rectangle shapes can be drawn and combined to create thermometer diagram.
pi_items.source: Web search results confirm ActivInspire has shape tools including Rectangle and Circle/Ellipse. Task description specifies: 'Draw a Circle (bulb) at the bottom and a tall Rectangle (stem) extending upward from it.'

pi_items.verified_value: Flipchart files are ZIP archives containing XML, as confirmed by task_utils.sh: 'Flipchart files are actually ZIP archives containing XML' (line 50). Files can be unpacked with unzip and examined for XML content containing text and shape definitions.
pi_items.source: Code analysis of task_utils.sh and export_result.sh which both use 'unzip' to extract and analyze .flipchart files. Multiple file-extension databases confirm .flipchart format.

privileged_info_summary: The weather_thermometer_lesson task requires creating a 2-page ActivInspire flipchart file saved as 'weather_tracker.flipchart' in /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/. The file format is a ZIP archive containing XML. Page 1 (Dashboard) must contain a thermometer diagram (Circle bulb + Rectangle stem), temperature labels (0, 32, 50, 70, 100), and weather conditions (Sunny, Rainy). Page 2 (Weekly Log) must display weekday headers and a horizontal line. The temperature values represent real reference points: 0°C and 100°C are Celsius freezing/boiling points, while 32°F is the Fahrenheit freezing point. However, the verifier only checks for Monday, Wednesday, and Friday labels despite the metadata claiming all five weekdays (Mon-Fri) are required.
